learning_outcomes.description: Characterise audiences based on their demographics, priorities, values, and communication preferences.
Develop and refine genre and purpose of engagement strategy based on your audience.
Develop and deliver a meaningful engagement strategy or activity that facilitates audience participation with science.
Evaluate the impact of an engagement strategy or activity.
Demonstrate proficiency in stakeholder engagement specific to science communication.

assessments.task_description:
You will produce an 8 to 10 page portfolio featuring your science engagement project in the community project.

There are three milestones in this assessment task (to keep you on task):

Milestone 1

The first 3 to 4 pages of your portfolio should:

·       introduce your project idea using a persuasive rationale,

·       characterise the audience you intend to work with based on their demographics, priorities, and communication preferences,

·       describe the scientific content to be communicated in your project, and

·       propose a thoughtful implementation plan.

You are expected to approach the development of your ideas in a scholarly manner, referencing scholarly work or other credible sources of information. Submission of Milestone 1 is optional. You should submit Milestone 1 by Week 5 if you would like feedback.

Milestone 2

The next 2 to 3 pages of your portfolio should:

·       demonstrate further development and refinement of your ideas that results in a clear activity plan, and

·       evidence instances where you demonstrated professional stakeholder engagement (e.g. contacting an external audience).

Submission of Milestone 2 is optional. You should submit Milestone 2 by Week 9 if you would like feedback.

Milestone 3

The final 3 to 4 pages of your portfolio should:

·       evidence genuine and authentic project delivery (e.g. photos, videos, creative outputs, testimonials etc.),

·       summarise and interpret audience feedback in a meaningful and constructive way, and

·       identify limitations and suggestions for improvement on your engagement activity based on your personal reflection and audience feedback.

Optional: you may supplement your project evaluation with any news stories, social media posts, or publications from your project supervisory team, industry partner, or external stakeholder company or organisation. You will submit Milestone 3 after study week (SWOTVAC).

The portfolio is designed to be a creative production that encourages use of mixed media. You could thoughtfully integrate written form, in-text citations, appropriate referencing style, visual art, photos, original short videos etc.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The first two Milestones can be emailed to your Course Coordinator for direct feedback.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Storytelling is a well-established technique in effective science communication. In Week 5 we will learn about storytelling techniques and an in-class assessment will require you to ideate a narrative for a chosen scientific concept. The purpose of this task is to assess your creative thinking skills, and you do not need to present a completed product at the end of the task, but rather evidence that you have employed an iterative process to your ideation. Further instructions will be provided in class.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
For at least one of the excursions, you will provide a 1 to 2 minute reflection presented in video format. You should be speaking authentically with minimal reference to notes or a script. The reflection should focus on what you have gained from the excursions that are directly relevant to your current or future employability.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission of video file will be direct to a Google Drive Link provided via Blackboard.

assessments.due_date:
23/10/2026 - 30/10/2026

You are expected to attended both workshops when projects are showcased (Weeks 12 and 13).
assessments.task_description:
You will present a summary and reflection of your science engagement project within the final two weeks of semester. There is flexibility in presentation mode and style, but your talk should not exceed 3 minutes in total duration. Further information will be provided in class.

Presentations will be recorded for marking purposes.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.topic:
Purpose (with Dr Gurion Ang)

Science engagement toolkit part 1 of 5

Welcome to SCIE3010! Communication of science vs science communication, community engagement principles and frameworks, and explanation of course assessment

Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.topic:
Visit to Integrated Pathology Learning Centre

Science engagement toolkit part 2 of 5

Excursion to UQ's Integrated Pathology Learning Centre (IPLC). Museum curation 101, challenges for engagement within a pathology museum, creating a safe space, science communication through exhibition design, object-based learning theory and essential practice, design principles of developing resources for biomedical science education

Please note that this excursion will take place on Thursday 6 August (during the usual Thursday timeslot for this course)

You will make your own way to IPLC at UQ Herston campus (M2 from UQ Lakes)

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Design Principles (with Associate Professor Louise Kuchel)

Science engagement toolkit part 3 of 5

Topics including but not limited to filling the GAP (genre, audience, purpose), brain science, memory and processing new information, values and beliefs, and framing messages.

learning_activities.topic:
Visit to Queensland Museum

Science engagement toolkit part 4 of 5

Excursion to Queensland Museum Kurilpa. Careers in science community engagement, public funding of museums, tour of curation facilities and infrastructure, observe constructivism and play-based learning in science communication (SparkLab)

Please note that this excursion will take place on Friday 21 August (not the usual Thursday timeslot for this course)

You will make your own way to QM (M2 from UQ Lakes)

learning_activities.topic:
Storytelling (with guest Dr Amanda Niehaus)

Science engagement toolkit part 5 of 5

Storytelling is a powerful tool in science communication that enhances engagement, understanding, and retention of scientific information. How fiction or personal non-fiction can engage reader empathy for and interest in scientific ideas and begin work on our own braided essay that connects science with our lives.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
